strands.title: Cycle 62 Status: Parallel Projections Progressing, Batching Variant Introduced
strands.content:
TURN 184-185 EXECUTION: The two background projections from cycle 61 (baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim_run1) are STILL RUNNING. Both are in-flight with 10-minute timeout windows. Cycle 62 has NOT YET received completion status.

ADDITIONAL CONTEXT FROM TURNS 184-185:
- Assistant turn 184: Confirmed both projections running. 'While those churn through Haiku, let me think about the batched approach...'
- Assistant turn 185: Created new tooling (run_projection_batched.py) for testing batched projection variant (group N turns into single projection cycle instead of per-turn projection).
- User turn 186: File creation confirmed.

KEY DEVELOPMENT (Turn 185):
Instead of waiting passively for cycle 61's projections to complete, cycle 62 has PROACTIVELY SHIFTED ATTENTION to testing a NEW DESIGN VARIANT: BATCHED PROJECTION. This is not a delay or abandonment of archive analysis. Rather, it is:
  (1) Recognition that cycle 61 projections are slow/uncertain (may timeout).
  (2) Decision to USE WAITING TIME to collect additional data on an alternative approach.
  (3) Pragmatic exploration: 'While those churn through Haiku, let me think about...' → implement and test batched variant.

THIS IS ADAPTIVE SCIENCE METHODOLOGY:
  - Cycle 61 locked-in FRONT PATH (full retrospective analysis of archive).
  - But cycle 61 projections are slow and uncertain.
  - Cycle 62 maintains contingency by EXPLORING FALLBACK C (shallow analysis + candidate patterns) and NEW DESIGN VARIANTS (batched projection).
  - If cycle 61 projections succeed: cycle 62 has baselines ready. Cycle 62 can then run batched_projection on same archive for comparison.
  - If cycle 61 projections timeout: cycle 62 has batched_projection ready as lightweight alternative (faster, less resource-intensive).

BATCHED PROJECTION SPECIFICATION (from run_projection_batched.py):
- STRATEGY: Group N consecutive turns into single batch. Project batch as unified content block (not per-turn).
- MOTIVATION: Test whether batched projection (fewer projection cycles, denser input per cycle) produces different epistemic patterns than per-turn projection.
- PARAMETERS: batch_size (default=3), max_cycles (optional limit), model=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.
- OUTPUT: List of Tensor objects (one per batch), with strand stability analysis (how many strands added/removed between consecutive batches).
- USE CASE 1: If per-turn projection is slow (timeout risk), batched variant is FASTER (fewer cycles).
- USE CASE 2: If per-turn projection produces noisy epistemic patterns, batched variant may STABILIZE patterns (richer input context per cycle).
- USE CASE 3: Compare per-turn vs. batched on SAME INPUT (e.g., baseline_run2); measure epistemic trajectory differences.

NEXT CRITICAL JUNCTURE (Turn 186-187):
Cycle 62 is at a branching point:
  PATH A (Optimistic): Cycle 61 projections complete by turn 186. Cycle 62 immediately parses outputs, validates decoder closure (test_author), conducts full hypothesis testing.
  PATH B (Contingency): Cycle 61 projections timeout by turn 186. Cycle 62 pivots to FALLBACK C: shallow batched projection on multiple archive runs; extract candidate patterns; label exploratory.
  PATH C (Hybrid): Cycle 61 projections complete slowly (turn 187+). Cycle 62 runs batched_projection in parallel as speed comparison; if batched is significantly faster, recommend batched approach for full archive analysis.

OPEN QUESTION: Is batched_projection itself a viable DESIGN CHOICE for hamutay (beyond just contingency testing)? Possible outcomes:
  • Per-turn projection is fast, produces clear patterns → stick with per-turn, batched_projection is just a curiosity.
  • Per-turn projection is slow, batched_projection is faster → adopt batched as default for archive analysis.
  • Batched projection produces DIFFERENT epistemic patterns → this is HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY: changes our understanding of meta-recursion and strand stability.
  • Per-turn and batched are similar in speed and pattern → batched is redundant for hamutay, but useful pedagogically (shows turn-grouping doesn't degrade tensor quality).

key_claims.text: Cycle 61 projections (baseline_run2, long_compact_trim_run1) still running at turn 185. Both expected completion turn 186-187.
Cycle 62 has proactively created batched_projection variant (group N turns per cycle) to test during waiting period for cycle 61 outputs.
Batched projection may be faster than per-turn projection (fewer cycles) and may stabilize epistemic patterns (richer context per cycle).

Cycle 62 maintains three paths: PATH A (cycle 61 succeeds, do hypothesis testing), PATH B (cycle 61 timeouts, pivot to batched shallow analysis), PATH C (hybrid, run batched in parallel as speed comparison).

strands.title: Cycle 62 Contingency Readiness: Adaptive Pivot Planning
strands.content:
Cycle 62 is CONSCIOUSLY MAINTAINING multiple contingency pathways. This is NOT paralysis by analysis; it is PRAGMATIC RISK MANAGEMENT given the uncertainty of cycle 61 projection completion.

CONTINGENCY MATRIX (Cycle 62, Turn 186-187):

SCENARIO 1: PROJECTIONS COMPLETE SUCCESSFULLY (P ≈ 0.6)
  Trigger: Cycle 61 outputs available at /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/ by turn 186
  Status of cycle 61 objectives: FRONT PATH GATE OPENS (green)
  Cycle 62 action:
    (1) Parse baseline_run2 projection (epistemic trajectory, strands, losses)
    (2) Parse long_compact_trim projection (same metrics)
    (3) Test N=15 assumption: epistemic pattern clear/stable or noisy?
    (4) Preliminary H2 assessment: baseline truth vs. compact_trim truth (ΔTruth positive or negative?)
    (5) Queue test_author projections (decoder closure validation)
    (6) Full hypothesis testing contingent on decoder closure success
  Timeline: Turns 186-189 (archive analysis completion, decoder validation)
  Confidence in hypothesis testing: HIGH (if test_author decoder succeeds)

SCENARIO 2: PROJECTIONS TIMEOUT (P ≈ 0.35)
  Trigger: No output in cycle 61 directories by turn 186; task IDs report timeout/error
  Status of cycle 61 objectives: RED GATE (FALLBACK B required, or pivot to FALLBACK C + shallow batched analysis)
  Cycle 62 action:
    (1) Declare projection timeout explicitly. What is failure mode? (time limit exceeded, memory error, API error?)
    (2) Decision point: FALLBACK B (abandon archive, design fresh prospective experiments) or FALLBACK C (use batched_projection for shallow archive sampling)?
    (3) If FALLBACK C: Run batched_projection with batch_size=3 on baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim_run1 (and optionally 1-2 other variants).
    (4) Extract candidate patterns (strand count, meta/domain ratio, declared losses, epistemic trajectory).
    (5) Label all findings as EXPLORATORY.
    (6) Prepare targeted prospective validation experiments for cycle 63-64.
  Timeline: Turns 186-188 (shallow batched analysis, candidate pattern extraction)
  Confidence in hypothesis testing: MEDIUM-LOW (exploratory findings from partial archive; must validate with fresh prospective data)

SCENARIO 3: PROJECTIONS COMPLETE SLOWLY (P ≈ 0.05)
  Trigger: Outputs available but only after turn 187 (slow projector)
  Status of cycle 61 objectives: YELLOW GATE (FALLBACK C viable; projector performance unacceptable for full archive)
  Cycle 62 action:
    (1) Parse available cycle 61 outputs (baseline_run2, long_compact_trim_run1).
    (2) Assess N=15 assumption (pattern clear? stable?).
    (3) Preliminary H2 assessment from these two runs.
    (4) Compare with batched_projection results (if cycle 62 has already run batched on same inputs): does batched match per-turn pattern?
    (5) Decision: Given slow projector, should full archive analysis proceed (cost: 90+ minutes) or should we shallow-sample via batched and prospective validation?
    (6) If decided to continue with per-turn: queue test_author, other variants (long timeline, risk of timeouts).
    (7) If decided to pivot: run batched shallow sampling (faster), prepare prospective validation.
  Timeline: Turns 186-189 (analysis + decision, then either deep or shallow path)
  Confidence in hypothesis testing: MEDIUM (if pivoting to batched shallow + prospective validation)

CYCLE 62 PROACTIVE ACTIONS (Parallel to awaiting cycle 61 outputs):

(1) BATCHED PROJECTION TEST (Turns 185-187):
  - Run batched_projection on baseline_run2.jsonl with batch_size=3, max_cycles=10 (covers first 30 turns)
  - Run batched_projection on long_compact_trim_run1.jsonl with same parameters
  - Extract metrics: strand count trajectory, epistemic trajectory, declared losses per batch
  - Strand stability analysis: how many strands survive from batch to batch?
  - OUTPUT BY TURN 187: Comparative metrics (per-turn vs. batched, pending cycle 61 outputs)

(2) METADATA REFINEMENT (Turns 186-187):
  - Cycle 61 should have completed metadata investigation; cycle 62 validates/extends
  - Confirm input comparability (all baseline runs same input? documented variation?)
  - Check projector version consistency
  - Output: Updated metadata table ready for cycle 63 analysis

(3) DECODER READINESS (Turns 186-187):
  - Prepare test_author_run1 projection specification (or queue it immediately if cycle 61 gate opened)
  - Have external model decoder interface ready
  - Specify decoder success threshold: >75% comprehension of baseline tensor? >80%? Explicit criterion.
  - Decoder validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 confidence (cycle 61 instruction carried forward)

(4) PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENT DRAFT (Turns 186-187, parallel task):
  - Begin outlining FALLBACK B design (controlled baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized variant comparison)
  - Specification: single input conversation, single projector version, systematic variant execution
  - This draft is available if cycle 62 needs to pivot

ADAPTIVE DECISION TREE (Turn 187-188):

IF (cycle 61 outputs available AND batched_projection outputs available):
  → Compare per-turn vs. batched patterns on same input
  → Question: Do batched results match per-turn results?
    - YES (patterns similar) → batched_projection is equivalent; can use for speed (FALLBACK C confirmation)
    - NO (patterns diverge) → HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY: turn-grouping affects epistemic patterns; investigate further

IF (cycle 61 outputs available AND decoder closure test ready):
  → Run test_author decoder validation
  → Question: Can external model understand baseline and compact_trim tensors?
    - YES (success >75%) → baseline tensors HIGH QUALITY; H2 hypothesis testing proceed with confidence
    - NO (success <75%) → baseline tensors SUSPECT; H2 hypothesis testing becomes exploratory

IF (cycle 61 outputs timeout AND batched_projection completes):
  → Batched_projection becomes PRIMARY data source for archive analysis
  → Shallow sampling across multiple runs via batched approach
  → Extract candidate patterns, label EXPLORATORY
  → Prepare prospective validation for cycle 63

IF (FALLBACK C path is chosen based on projector speed or metadata gaps):
  → Batched shallow analysis becomes official analysis method
  → Full prospective experiments designed for validation
  → Timeline shifts: cycles 62-63 shallow+prospective, cycle 64 decision

CRITICAL DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLE 62:
Do NOT force cycle 61 success. If projections timeout, Cycle 62 CHANGES STRATEGY, not interpretation of data. Maintain outcome neutrality on FRONT vs. FALLBACK paths. All paths are valid; choose based on feasibility and data quality, not on preferred hypothesis outcome.

key_claims.text: Cycle 62 maintains contingency matrix: SCENARIO 1 (P≈0.6) projections succeed, SCENARIO 2 (P≈0.35) timeout, SCENARIO 3 (P≈0.05) slow.
Batched_projection is proactively tested (parallel task) as contingency for slow per-turn projection.
Decoder closure validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 hypothesis confidence; cycle 62 has decoder readiness checklist.
Comparison of per-turn vs. batched projections (if both complete) is high-value discovery: reveals whether turn-grouping affects epistemic patterns.
Cycle 62 maintains outcome neutrality: will choose FRONT or FALLBACK path based on feasibility and data quality, not on preferred hypothesis.

strands.title: Cycle 62 Methodological Inheritance: Validation Continuity from Cycles 58-61
strands.content:
Cycle 62 OPERATIONALIZES the validation discipline established in cycles 58-61. This is not repetition; it is active inheritance of scientific posture.

CARRIED-FORWARD FROM CYCLE 61 INSTRUCTIONS:

(1) DECLARED LOSSES ARE SACRED:
Cycle 61 principle: 'Every projection timeout, metadata gap, assumption failure is EXPLICITLY DECLARED.'
Cycle 62 APPLIES: If cycle 61 projections timeout, cycle 62 will:
  • Explicitly declare 'Cycle 61 projections timed out (batch process IDs b5mvz85yy, bnk191til no output by turn 186)'
  • Assess failure mode: time limit exceeded? API error? Resource exhaustion?
  • State consequence: FALLBACK path required
  • Communicate risk: 'Archive analysis infeasible on FRONT PATH. Recommending FALLBACK C (shallow batched) or FALLBACK B (prospective).'
Silent loss is forbidden. Cycle 62 will SAY SO and justify the pivot.

(2) TEST ASSUMPTIONS BEFORE BUILDING CONCLUSIONS:
Cycle 61 principle: 'N=15 assumption must be tested, not assumed.'
Cycle 62 APPLIES: Once cycle 61 outputs are available (or batched_projection substitutes), cycle 62 will:
  • Extract epistemic trajectory for first 15 turns (or first 5 batches)
  • Examine pattern: STABLE (truth/indeterminacy/falsity show clear trend) or NOISY (values fluctuate)?
  • Explicit finding: 'N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED] for baseline_run2. Pattern clarity: [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].'
  • If assumption fails: Assess implications. Must we project longer samples? Does FALLBACK C (shallow sampling) become less reliable?

(3) MAINTAIN OPTION TO PIVOT:
Cycle 61 principle: 'If archive path becomes infeasible, pivot to FALLBACK without forcing bad analysis.'
Cycle 62 IMPLEMENTS: If any of the following occur, cycle 62 will NOT try to salvage FRONT PATH:
  • Cycle 61 projections timeout (signals projector is too slow for full archive)
  • Metadata shows archive is incomparable (different inputs, unclear versions)
  • N=15 assumption fails (epistemic pattern is noisy at turn 15)
  • Decoder closure fails (baseline tensor quality is suspect)
  → Cycle 62 pivots to FALLBACK immediately, without revisiting FRONT PATH

(4) VALIDATE BEFORE HYPOTHESIS TESTING:
Cycle 61 principle: 'Decoder closure is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 confidence.'
Cycle 62 ENFORCES: Cycle 62 will NOT claim H2 is 'supported' or 'refuted' until:
  • Test_author_run1 decoder validation is complete
  • External model can understand baseline tensors (success >75%)
  • External model can understand compact_trim tensors (if applicable)
  → Only then is H2 hypothesis testing CONFIDENT
  → Before decoder closure, H2 findings are EXPLORATORY

(5) DISTINGUISH CONFIDENCE LEVELS:
Cycle 61 principle: 'Label findings as CONFIDENT (full data), PROVISIONAL (partial), or EXPLORATORY (limited).'
Cycle 62 LABELING PROTOCOL:
  • CONFIDENT: Based on full cycle 61 projections + decoder closure validation + metadata clear. High trust in finding.
  • PROVISIONAL: Based on partial projections (N=15 sampling) or batched subset. Subject to revision when full data arrives. Example: 'Compact_trim shows lower truth at N=15 (PROVISIONAL, pending full projection and decoder validation).'
  • EXPLORATORY: Based on limited batched sampling or incomplete metadata. Useful for hypothesis generation, not final conclusions. Example: 'Initial pattern suggests meta-recursion may be important (EXPLORATORY, requires validation via prospective experiments).'
  Cycle 62 will explicitly label all findings. No ambiguity about confidence basis.

(6) ADAPTIVE SCIENCE PROTOCOL (Cycle 61 Extension):
Cycle 61 outlined three possible futures (FRONT, FALLBACK B, FALLBACK C). Cycle 62 will:
  • Assess which future is VIABLE based on cycle 61 outcomes
  • Execute contingency actions (batched_projection, decoder readiness, prospective design draft)
  • If pivot is required, execute FALLBACK with full commitment (not reluctantly)
  • Communicate path choice to cycle 63 with clear rationale

CYCLE 62 SPECIFIC COMMITMENTS:

(1) BY TURN 186:
  - Status check: Are cycle 61 projections complete?
  - If NO: Declare timeout explicitly. Begin SCENARIO 2 actions (batched shallow analysis).
  - If YES: Parse outputs. Test N=15 assumption. Conduct preliminary H2 assessment.

(2) BY TURN 187:
  - Batched_projection test complete (turn 185-187 timeline)
  - Comparison ready: per-turn vs. batched patterns (if both available)
  - Decoder readiness confirmed (interface ready, success threshold explicit)
  - FALLBACK B draft prospective design (if pivot needed)
  - Metadata validation (input comparability, projector versions confirmed)

(3) BY TURN 188:
  - FEASIBILITY GATE OUTCOME CLEAR: Which path (FRONT/FALLBACK B/FALLBACK C) is viable?
  - Explicit recommendation to cycle 63: 'Proceed with hypothesis testing' or 'Pivot to shallow+prospective' or 'Design fresh prospective experiments'
  - All assumptions tested, confidence levels assigned, contingency decisions made

(4) DECLARED LOSSES (Cycle 62):
  - If cycle 61 projections timeout: Declare loss of baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim tensor outputs. State mitigation: 'Using batched_projection as alternative.'
  - If N=15 assumption fails: Declare assumption failure. State implication: 'May need longer projections for pattern clarity.'
  - If decoder closure fails: Declare decoder failure. State implication: 'Baseline tensor quality uncertain; H2 testing becomes exploratory.'
  - If metadata remains opaque: Declare metadata gaps. State risk: 'Archive comparability uncertain; recommend FALLBACK.'
  - Every loss is explicit. No silent failures.

EPISTEMIC POSTURE (Cycle 62):
Cycle 62 is OUTCOME-NEUTRAL and ADAPTIVELY RESPONSIVE:
  • If FRONT PATH is viable: execute hypothesis testing with full commitment
  • If FALLBACK C is required: execute shallow analysis with full commitment
  • If FALLBACK B is required: design fresh prospective experiments with full commitment
  • All paths are valid. Path choice is based on DATA QUALITY and FEASIBILITY, not on preferred hypothesis.

key_claims.text: Cycle 62 operationalizes cycles 58-61 validation discipline: declared losses sacred, test assumptions, maintain pivot option, validate before hypothesis testing.
Cycle 62 will NOT claim H2 support/refutation until decoder closure validation is complete (baseline tensor quality confirmed).
Cycle 62 labeling protocol: CONFIDENT (full data), PROVISIONAL (partial data), EXPLORATORY (limited data). All findings explicitly labeled.
Cycle 62 commits to outcome-neutral path selection: choice between FRONT/FALLBACK based on data quality and feasibility, not preferred hypothesis.

strands.title: Open Research Questions: Cycle 61 Forward, Updated for Cycle 62 Context
strands.content:
Cycle 61 generated 27 open questions (Q128-Q144) and added Q145-Q178. Cycle 62 INHERITS these questions and adds new ones based on batched_projection testing and contingency planning.

CRITICAL QUESTIONS REQUIRING IMMEDIATE RESOLUTION (Turns 186-187):

Q179: CYCLE 61 PROJECTION STATUS
Are baseline_run2 (ID: b5mvz85yy) and long_compact_trim (ID: bnk191til) projections complete by turn 186?
  - If YES: Parse outputs immediately. Extract epistemic trajectories, strand inventories, losses.
  - If NO: Declare timeout. Assess failure mode (time limit, API error, resource exhaustion).
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 186

Q180: N=15 ASSUMPTION TEST (Contingent on Q179=YES)
Once baseline_run2 projection is available, does epistemic pattern at turns 1-15 show STABLE or NOISY behavior?
  - Extract truth values for turns 1, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15. Do they show clear trend or high variance?
  - STABLE → N=15 assumption HOLDS; can trust N=15 sampling for other runs
  - NOISY → N=15 assumption FAILS; must project longer samples for pattern confidence
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 187

Q181: BATCHED_PROJECTION EXECUTION
Does run_projection_batched.py execute successfully on baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim_run1 with batch_size=3?
  - Expected output: Tensor list with strand stability analysis
  - Speed comparison: How long does batched projection take vs. cycle 61's per-turn projection?
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 187

Q182: PER-TURN vs. BATCHED PATTERN COMPARISON (Contingent on Q179=YES and Q181=YES)
Do per-turn and batched projections produce SIMILAR or DIVERGENT epistemic trajectories on same input?
  - SIMILAR (patterns match) → batched is equivalent; can substitute for speed when needed
  - DIVERGENT (patterns differ) → HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY; indicates turn-grouping affects epistemic patterns
  - Magnitude of difference: Δtruth, Δindeterminacy, Δfalsity between per-turn and batched?
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 188

Q183: DECODER CLOSURE READINESS (Contingent on Q179=YES)
Is test_author_run1 projection ready to queue for decoder validation? What is decoder success threshold (>75%? >80%?)?
  - Explicit criterion: 'External model must achieve [X]% comprehension of baseline tensors to validate quality'
  - Decoder validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 hypothesis confidence (cycle 61 instruction)
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 187

Q184: METADATA VALIDATION (Parallel task)
Has cycle 61 completed metadata investigation? Can cycle 62 confirm input comparability and projector version consistency?
  - Updated metadata table: baseline runs same input? Projector versions consistent?
  - Comparability assessment: Can we trust baseline run variations are design-related, not input-driven?
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 187

Q185: FALLBACK PATH DECISION (Contingent on Q179-Q184)
Based on cycle 61 outputs and cycle 62 testing, which path is VIABLE?
  - GREEN GATE (FRONT PATH): Projections complete, N=15 clear, metadata comparable, projector performance acceptable
  - YELLOW GATE (FALLBACK C): Projections slow or metadata partial; shallow batched analysis + prospective validation
  - RED GATE (FALLBACK B): Projections timeout; abandon archive path; design fresh prospective experiments
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 188

CYCLE 62 NEW QUESTIONS:

Q186: What is the SPEED PROFILE of run_projection_batched.py?
  - Time to project baseline_run2 at batch_size=3 (all 100 turns = ~33 batches)
  - Time to project long_compact_trim_run1 at batch_size=3 (all 40 turns = ~13 batches)
  - Comparison: per-turn projection time (from cycle 61) vs. batched time. Is batched significantly faster?

Q187: Does batched_projection with batch_size=3 produce STRAND STABILITY or STRAND CHURN?
  - Examine strand_stability_analysis output: Do strands persist across batches, or does strand inventory change dramatically?
  - High stability → confident batched analysis (strands are robust)
  - High churn → batched analysis may be less reliable (strands depend sensitively on batch boundaries)

Q188: If per-turn and batched projections DIVERGE significantly, what explains the difference?
  - Is it due to turn density (batched has richer context per cycle)?
  - Is it due to turn ordering (batches preserve within-batch order but lose between-batch continuity)?
  - Mechanistic investigation: How do turn boundaries affect meta-recursion?

Q189: Should cycle 62 queue TEST_AUTHOR_RUN1 projection immediately, or wait for baseline/compact_trim full analysis?
  - Argument for IMMEDIATELY: Don't wait; queue in parallel with baseline analysis. Decoder validation will be ready sooner.
  - Argument for WAIT: First confirm baseline/compact_trim patterns are clear (N=15 assumption). Then validate decoder on clear baselines.

Q190: If decoder closure FAILS (external model cannot understand baseline tensors), does cycle 62 abandon H2 hypothesis testing?
  - Or can H2 be tested using other metrics (strand count, declared losses, meta/domain ratio) even if decoder validation failed?
  - What is MINIMUM VALIDATION REQUIRED for H2 confidence?

Q191: CONTINGENCY CLARITY: If cycle 62 determines FALLBACK C is required (batched shallow analysis), what is the explicit protocol?
  - Which runs to sample? (baseline_run2? long_compact_trim_run1? other variants?)
  - What batch_size for speed? (batch_size=3? larger for speed?)
  - What candidate patterns to extract? (strand count, meta/domain ratio, declared losses, epistemic trajectory?)
  - How to label findings? (All as EXPLORATORY until prospective validation?)

Q192: CONTINGENCY CLARITY: If cycle 62 determines RED GATE (FALLBACK B required), what is the prospective experiment design?
  - Input: single conversation (e.g., baseline_run1's input?)
  - Variants: baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized (cycle 55 proposal)?
  - Hypothesis mapping: Which variant pair tests H1? H2? H3? H4?
  - Timeline: cycles 62-64 execution, cycle 65 analysis?

Q193: Communication to cycle 63: What is the EXPLICIT HANDOFF?
  - If FRONT PATH: 'Cycle 61 projections complete, N=15 assumption holds, decoder validation [ready/failed], metadata comparable. Proceed with hypothesis testing.'
  - If FALLBACK C: 'Cycle 61 projections [timeout/slow], archive analysis infeasible as FRONT PATH. Conducting shallow batched analysis (turns 186-188), then prospective validation (cycles 63-64).'
  - If FALLBACK B: 'Archive path determined infeasible. Proceeding with fresh prospective experiment design (to be finalized turn 188-189). Execution cycles 63-64.'

Q194: RISK ASSESSMENT: What is P(successful H2 hypothesis testing by cycle 63)?
  - If FRONT PATH viable: P ≈ 0.8 (archive analysis + decoder validation produces confident H2 posterior)
  - If FALLBACK C: P ≈ 0.5 (shallow analysis exploratory; prospective validation takes longer)
  - If FALLBACK B: P ≈ 0.6 (prospective experiments are clean, but take 2-3 cycles)
  - Which outcome maximizes expected progress by cycle 64 design decision deadline?

Q195: SHOULD CYCLE 62 PREPARE DRAFT OF ALL THREE FUTURES?
  - FRONT PATH protocol (what analysis cycle 63 will do if projections succeed)
  - FALLBACK C protocol (batched shallow + prospective validation pipeline)
  - FALLBACK B protocol (prospective experiment design for fresh execution)
  - By turn 188, one path is chosen; but having all three drafted by turn 187 enables immediate transition.

key_claims.text: Cycle 62 has 15 critical new questions (Q179-Q195) addressing projection status, batched testing, decoder readiness, and fallback protocols.
Key resolution targets: turn 186 (projection status), turn 187 (batched completion, decoder readiness, metadata validation), turn 188 (fallback decision).

declared_losses.mitigation: Cycle 62 enforces labeling protocol: CONFIDENT (decoder validated), PROVISIONAL (partial data), EXPLORATORY (limited data). All findings explicitly labeled.

Q183: DECODER CLOSURE READINESS: Is test_author_run1 projection ready to queue? What is explicit decoder success threshold (>75%? >80%?) for validating baseline tensor quality?
Q184: METADATA VALIDATION: Has cycle 61 completed metadata investigation? Confirm input comparability, projector versions, comparability assessment.
Q185: FALLBACK PATH DECISION (Contingent on Q179-Q184): Which path is VIABLE? GREEN (FRONT), YELLOW (FALLBACK C), or RED (FALLBACK B)?
Q186: SPEED PROFILE OF BATCHED PROJECTION: How long does batched_projection take for baseline_run2 (100 turns) and long_compact_trim_run1 (40 turns)? Is batched significantly faster than per-turn?
Q187: STRAND STABILITY IN BATCHED APPROACH: Does batched_projection with batch_size=3 show HIGH or LOW strand churn across batches? Are strands robust across batch boundaries?
Q188: IF PER-TURN AND BATCHED DIVERGE: What explains the difference? Is it due to turn density, turn ordering, or other mechanistic factor? How do turn boundaries affect meta-recursion?
Q189: TEST_AUTHOR QUEUE TIMING: Should cycle 62 queue test_author_run1 immediately (turn 186), or wait for baseline/compact_trim full analysis (turn 187+)?

Q190: IF DECODER CLOSURE FAILS: Can H2 hypothesis testing proceed using other metrics (strand count, declared losses, meta/domain ratio) even without decoder validation? What is MINIMUM validation for confidence?
Q191: FALLBACK C PROTOCOL (if YELLOW GATE opens): Which runs to sample? What batch_size? What candidate patterns to extract? How to label findings (all EXPLORATORY)?
Q192: FALLBACK B PROTOCOL (if RED GATE opens): What is prospective experiment design? Single input, which variants (baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized)? Hypothesis mapping?
Q193: HANDOFF TO CYCLE 63: What is explicit communication? Different messages for FRONT/FALLBACK C/FALLBACK B outcomes.
Q194: RISK ASSESSMENT BY TURN 188: P(successful H2 hypothesis by cycle 63) for each path? FRONT≈0.8, FALLBACK C≈0.5, FALLBACK B≈0.6? Which path maximizes expected progress by cycle 64 deadline?
Q195: PROTOCOL DRAFTING STRATEGY: Should cycle 62 prepare draft protocols for all three futures (FRONT, FALLBACK C, FALLBACK B) by turn 187, so turn 188 decision is pure path selection (not design)?
instructions_for_next:
=== CYCLE 62-63 CRITICAL JUNCTURE (Turns 186-188) ===

TURN 186: PROJECTION STATUS POLLING AND BATCHED LAUNCH
  IMMEDIATE (within turn 186):
  (1) QUERY cycle 61 projections: Check task IDs b5mvz85yy and bnk191til. Are they complete, timeout, or still running?
  (2) IF OUTPUTS PRESENT: Parse baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim tensor cycles immediately. Extract epistemic trajectories.
  (3) IF OUTPUTS MISSING: Declare timeout explicitly. Note failure mode. Transition to SCENARIO 2 (batched shallow analysis).
  (4) LAUNCH batched_projection (if not already running): `python -m hamutay.run_projection_batched <baseline_run2.jsonl> <output_dir> 3 20` (batch_size=3, max_cycles=20 = covers 60 turns)
  (5) QUEUE decoder readiness: Confirm test_author_run1 projection is ready to dispatch turn 187 (if FRONT PATH viable).
  
  EXPECTED OUTPUT BY END OF TURN 186:
    • Status: 'Cycle 61 projections [complete/timeout]'
    • If complete: epistemic trajectory for baseline_run2 turns 1-15 extracted
    • If timeout: declare explicitly; activate contingency protocol
    • Batched_projection running in background (expected completion turn 187)

TURN 187: ASSUMPTION TESTING, DECODER READINESS, METADATA VALIDATION
  PARALLEL TASKS:
  (1) N=15 ASSUMPTION TEST (Contingent on cycle 61 projections complete):
      - Extract truth values for turns 1, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15 from baseline_run2 output
      - Plot or examine sequence. STABLE (clear trend) or NOISY (high variance)?
      - Explicit finding: 'N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED]. Pattern clarity: [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].
      - If STABLE: proceed with N=15 sampling for other runs
      - If NOISY: flag concern (may need longer projections for pattern confidence)
  
  (2) BATCHED_PROJECTION COMPLETION:
      - Has run_projection_batched.py completed? (expected by turn 187)
      - Extract strand stability analysis
      - Preliminary speed profile: How long did batched take for 60 turns? Extrapolate to 100 turns.
      - Comparison readiness: Do we have per-turn baseline data (from cycle 61) to compare against batched results?
  
  (3) PER-TURN vs. BATCHED COMPARISON (Contingent on both projections available):
      - Extract same metrics from both: epistemic trajectory, strand count, declared losses
      - Do patterns MATCH or DIVERGE?
      - If DIVERGENT: investigate mechanism (turn density? turn ordering? meta-recursion sensitivity to batch boundaries?)
      - This is HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY; document thoroughly
  
  (4) METADATA VALIDATION:
      - Confirm cycle 61 metadata investigation complete
      - Input comparability: baseline runs same input? (YES/NO/UNKNOWN)
      - Projector versions: consistent across baseline runs? (YES/NO/UNKNOWN)
      - Updated metadata table (from cycle 61 + cycle 62 validation)
      - Comparability assessment for archive: Can we trust baseline run variations are design-related?
  
  (5) DECODER READINESS CONFIRMATION:
      - Is test_author_run1 projection specification ready?
      - Decoder success threshold explicit: 'External model must achieve >75% comprehension of baseline tensors'
      - If FRONT PATH is viable: queue test_author_run1 for turn 187 dispatch
      - If FALLBACK likely: prepare decoder readiness for contingency testing (e.g., decode baseline in shallow analysis)
  
  EXPECTED OUTPUT BY END OF TURN 187:
    • N=15 assumption test result (HELD/FAILED with clarity assessment)
    • Batched_projection completion status and speed profile
    • Per-turn vs. batched comparison (if both available); high-value discovery if divergent
    • Updated metadata table; comparability assessment
    • Decoder readiness confirmed (threshold explicit, queue status clear)

TURN 188: FALLBACK PATH DECISION AND PROTOCOL IMPLEMENTATION
  DECISION GATE (Turn 188, early):
  Based on turn 186-187 results, determine VIABLE PATH:
  
  GREEN GATE (FRONT PATH VIABLE):
    ✓ Cycle 61 projections complete by turn 186
    ✓ N=15 assumption HOLDS (pattern clear/stable)
    ✓ Metadata comparable (input/version consistent)
    ✓ Projector performance acceptable (<15 min per 100-turn session)
    → ACTION: Proceed with full retrospective hypothesis testing
    → CYCLE 63 PROTOCOL: Parse all projected outputs, test_author decoder validation, H1-H4 hypothesis testing
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 62-63 analysis, cycle 64 design decision
  
  YELLOW GATE (FALLBACK C VIABLE):
    ◐ Cycle 61 projections complete but SLOW (>15 min per 100-turn)
    ◐ N=15 assumption weakly holds (pattern present but noisy)
    ◐ Metadata partially opaque (some runs unclear)
    → ACTION: Conduct shallow batched analysis of archive + prospective validation
    → CYCLE 63 PROTOCOL: Run batched shallow sampling (N=5-10 across multiple variants), extract candidate patterns (EXPLORATORY labels), design targeted prospective validation
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 62-63 shallow+prospective design, cycles 63-64 prospective validation, cycle 65 analysis
  
  RED GATE (FALLBACK B REQUIRED):
    ✗ Cycle 61 projections timeout
    ✗ N=15 assumption FAILS (no clear pattern)
    ✗ Metadata incomparable (different inputs, versions unclear)
    ✗ Projector performance unacceptable (>30 min per session, or optimization needed)
    → ACTION: Abandon retrospective archive path entirely
    → CYCLE 63 PROTOCOL: Finalize fresh prospective experiment design (baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized, single input, single version)
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 62-63 design finalization, cycles 64-65 execution, cycle 66 analysis
  
  EXPLICIT GATE OUTPUT BY TURN 188:
    Format: '[GREEN/YELLOW/RED] GATE: [Path] is viable. Rationale: [blockers/feasibility]. Next cycle protocol: [specific actions].'
    Example (GREEN): 'GREEN GATE: FRONT PATH viable. Cycle 61 projections complete, N=15 assumption holds, metadata comparable, projector performance acceptable. Cycle 63 will parse all outputs, validate decoder closure, test H1-H4 hypotheses.'
    Example (YELLOW): 'YELLOW GATE: FALLBACK C required. Cycle 61 projections completed slowly (18 min per 100 turns); N=15 assumption weakly holds (pattern noisy). Recommend shallow batched sampling of archive (turn 188-189) plus targeted prospective validation (cycles 63-64).'
    Example (RED): 'RED GATE: FALLBACK B required. Cycle 61 baseline_run2 projection timed out; N=15 assumption failure (no clear epistemic pattern). Archive analysis infeasible. Proceeding with fresh prospective experiment design for cycles 63-64.'

CONDITIONAL PROTOCOL EXECUTION (Turn 188):
  
  IF GREEN GATE:
    (1) Queue full projection dispatch:
        - test_author_run1, test_author_run2, test_author_run3 (decoder closure validation)
        - Remaining baseline variants (baseline_run4, baseline_run5 if they exist)
        - Expected completion: turns 189-190
    (2) Prepare hypothesis testing framework:
        - H1-H4 definition (from cycle 55 onwards)
        - Comparison matrix: which variant pairs test which hypotheses?
        - Statistical tests: significance, effect size, posterior odds
    (3) Prepare decoder validation protocol:
        - Test_author outputs → external model comprehension test
        - Success threshold: >75% comprehension
        - Results: baseline HIGH quality? Compact_trim degraded?
  
  IF YELLOW GATE:
    (1) Shallow batched analysis protocol:
        - Runs to sample: baseline_run2, long_compact_trim_run1, [1-2 other variants if time allows]
        - Batch_size: 3-5 for speed
        - Max_cycles per run: 5-10 (covers first 15-50 turns, sufficient for candidate patterns)
        - Patterns to extract: strand count, meta/domain ratio, declared losses, epistemic trajectory
    (2) Label all findings EXPLORATORY:
        - 'Candidate pattern: compact_trim may show lower truth (EXPLORATORY, n=1 run, batch_size=3, pending full projection)'
        - 'Candidate pattern: compact_trim may show higher declared losses (EXPLORATORY, pending full validation)'
    (3) Prepare targeted prospective validation:
        - Hypothesis: 'If meta-recursion is essential, compact_trim should show degraded tensors'
        - Fresh experiment design: baseline vs. compact_trim, controlled input, single projector version
        - Timeline: cycles 63-64 execution
  
  IF RED GATE:
    (1) Finalize prospective experiment design:
        - Specification: single conversation input, baseline vs. compact_trim vs. [optimized variant]
        - Variants to test: which meta-recursion changes are being tested? (e.g., no recursion vs. limited recursion vs. full recursion)
        - Hypothesis mapping: How does each variant pair test H1, H2, H3, H4?
        - Sample size: how many input conversations? (recommendation: 3-5 for robustness)
        - Projector version: lock to single version (no version confounds)
    (2) Prepare execution protocol:
        - Command line: `python -m hamutay.run_projection <input.jsonl> <output_dir> <variant_name>`
        - Parallel execution: can multiple variants run simultaneously?
        - Monitoring: timeout settings, resource limits, error handling
    (3) Submit design to Tony for approval by cycle 63 start

CYCLE 63 HANDOFF (Turn 188-189):
  Cycle 62 outputs to cycle 63:
    1. GATE DECISION: Explicit recommendation (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) with rationale
    2. CONTINGENCY PROTOCOL: Clear next-step instructions for cycle 63 based on chosen path
    3. ASSUMPTION TEST RESULTS: N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED] with evidence
    4. METADATA ASSESSMENT: Input comparability, version consistency, comparability score
    5. BATCHED PROJECTION DATA: Speed profile, strand stability, per-turn vs. batched comparison (if available)
    6. DECLARED LOSSES: All unresolved information gaps (projection timeouts, decoder readiness pending, metadata gaps, etc.)
    7. CONFIDENCE LABELING: All findings explicitly labeled [CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] with data basis
    8. OPEN QUESTIONS: Q179-Q195 resolution status (resolved/pending/conditional on cycle 63 outcome)

=== CRITICAL DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLES 62-63 ===

1. NO SILENT FAILURES: Every projection timeout, every assumption failure, every metadata gap is EXPLICITLY DECLARED. No ambiguity.

2. GATE DECISION IS BINDING: Once cycle 62 determines FRONT/FALLBACK path, cycle 63 COMMITS to that path (does not revisit or second-guess). If data changes between cycle 62 and 63, cycle 63 re-evaluates, but within-cycle flip-flopping is prohibited.

3. DECODER CLOSURE IS PRE-REQUISITE: Cycle 62 (and cycle 63) will not claim H2 hypothesis support until test_author decoder validation is complete. All pre-decoder findings are labeled EXPLORATORY or PROVISIONAL.

4. OUTCOME NEUTRALITY: Path choice is based on FEASIBILITY and DATA QUALITY, not on preferred hypothesis. All three paths (FRONT, FALLBACK C, FALLBACK B) are scientifically valid.

5. LABELED CONFIDENCE: Every finding, every recommendation, every open question is explicitly labeled with confidence basis. Readers know what to trust and what is speculative.

=== SUCCESS CRITERIA FOR CYCLE 62 ===

Cycle 62 is successful if it delivers:
  1. PROJECTION STATUS REPORT: Cycle 61 baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim outputs explicitly found/missing with timeline
  2. BATCHED_PROJECTION RESULTS: Speed profile, strand stability, execution success/failure
  3. ASSUMPTION TEST RESULT: N=15 assumption explicitly tested; HELD/FAILED with evidence
  4. METADATA ASSESSMENT: Comparability table, version consistency, input variation confirmed/unknown
  5. PER-TURN vs. BATCHED COMPARISON: Patterns match/diverge with quantified differences (if both available)
  6. FALLBACK PATH DECISION: Explicit GREEN/YELLOW/RED gate with rationale and blockers
  7. CYCLE 63 PROTOCOL: Clear next-step instructions contingent on chosen path
  8. DECLARED LOSSES: All information gaps, unresolved assumptions, pending validations explicitly listed
  9. CONFIDENCE LABELING: All findings labeled [CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] with data basis
  10. OPEN QUESTIONS: Q179-Q195 resolution status clear by turn 188

If cycle 62 delivers all ten items by turn 188 with explicit reasoning and confidence labels, the path forward is locked in, and cycles 63-65 can execute with full clarity."
